title: The Big Bang
puzzle: If the universe began with the Big Bang, where did it actually happen?
insight: The Big Bang was not an explosion inside empty space. It was the beginning of space expanding everywhere, so every region in the cosmos shares the same starting event.
key_ideas: The universe began about 13.8 billion years ago.
Expansion means space itself has stretched over time.
Stars and galaxies formed later as matter cooled and gathered.
mind_blower: There is no central point in space you could visit to find where the Big Bang "went off."
next_curiosity: If space has been expanding ever since, how do astronomers measure that motion?
narration_text: If the universe began with the Big Bang, where did it actually happen? The Big Bang was not an explosion inside empty space. It was the beginning of space expanding everywhere, so every region in the cosmos shares the same starting event. The universe began about 13.8 billion years ago. Expansion means space itself has stretched over time. Stars and galaxies formed later as matter cooled and gathered. There is no central point in space you could visit to find where the Big Bang "went off." If space has been expanding ever since, how do astronomers measure that motion?
